sections.heading: (1) These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection
sections.summary: This section establishes the title of the Regulations as the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations, 2021. It provides the formal citation for the legislative instrument without imposing any operational requirements.

requirements.text: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations, 2021

sections.source_quote: These Regulations may be cited as the Data Protection (Registration of Data Controllers and Data Processors) Regulations, 2021

sections.heading: In these Regulations, unless the context otherwise requires
sections.summary: This section defines 'establishment' as the legal instruments or structures that create a body, including statutes, charters, registration certificates, trust deeds, and governing administrative frameworks. It clarifies the scope of entities that can be registered under the Regulations.

requirements.text: establishment means the person appointed under section 6 of the Act
establishment (a) a Statute, Charter or statutory instrument in which a body is established
establishment (b) registration certificate
establishment (c) trust deeds in which a trust has been established
establishment (d) other instruments by which a body is established including its governing and administrative structure

sections.source_quote: establishment means the person appointed under section 6 of the Act; (a) a Statute, Charter or statutory instrument in which a body is established; (b) registration certificate; (c) trust deeds in which a trust has been established; and (d) other instruments by which a body is established including its governing and administrative structure.

sections.heading: (1) These Regulations provide for the procedure for
sections.summary: This section specifies that the Regulations govern the registration procedure for data controllers and data processors as outlined in section 18 of the Act, excluding civil registration entities covered by the Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations.

requirements.text: These Regulations provide for the procedure for registration of data controllers and data processors as provided under section 18 of the Act
These Regulations shall not apply to civil registration entities specified under the Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations

sections.source_quote: These Regulations provide for the procedure for registration of data controllers and data processors as provided under section 18 of the Act. These Regulations shall not apply to civil registration entities specified under the Data Protection (Civil Registration) Regulations,

sections.heading: (1) Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data
sections.summary: This section mandates that all data controllers and processors must register under the Regulations, with specific criteria for determining whether a person is a data controller or processor. It allows data controllers to apply for dual registration and clarifies that processors acting outside instructions become controllers for that activity.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes registration requirements for data controllers and processors, which are critical for DTIA as it defines the legal framework for identifying and classifying data handling entities before cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data processor shall be required to register in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations
For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data controller, where the person determines the purpose and means for processing personal data

For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data processor, where the person processes personal data on behalf of the data controller but excludes employees of the data controller and has (i) a contractual relationship with the data controller; and (ii) no decision making power on the purpose and means of processing personal data

Despite sub-regulation (2) (a), a data controller may apply for registration as both a data controller and a data processor with regards to any processing operations and shall be required to pay the requisite fees applicable for both a data controller and a data processor thereto

Despite sub-regulation (2) (b), where a data processor processes personal data other than as instructed by the data controller, the data processor shall be considered to be a data controller in respect of that processing activity, for purposes of assessing liability

sections.source_quote: Subject to regulation 13 (1), every data controller and data processor shall be required to register in accordance with the provisions of the Act and these Regulations. For purposes of registration, a person shall register as a data controller, where the person determines the purpose and means for processing personal data; or a data processor, where the person processes personal data on behalf of the data controller but excludes employees of the data controller and has (i) a contractual relationship with the data controller; and (ii) no decision making power on the purpose and means of processing personal data.

sections.heading: (1) An application for registration of a data controller or data
sections.summary: This section outlines the application requirements for registration, including specific forms, fees, and documentation such as establishment documents, personal data descriptions, and processing purposes.
sections.dtia_summary: This section details the documentation required for registration, which is essential for DTIA as it ensures that data handlers have properly documented their data processing activities before cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: An application for registration of a data controller or data processor shall be in Form DPR1 set out in the First Schedule
An application for registration of a data controller or data processor shall be accompanied by the registration fees specified in the Second Schedule
An application for registration under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by a copy of the establishment documents
An application for registration under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by particulars of the data controllers or data processors including name and contact details
An application for registration under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by a description of the purpose for which personal data is processed
An application for registration under sub-regulation (1) shall be accompanied by a description of categories of personal data being processed

sections.source_quote: An application for registration of a data controller or data processor shall be in Form DPR1 set out in the First Schedule; and be accompanied by the registration fees specified in the Second Schedule.

sections.heading: (1) A state department or county department shall register and
sections.summary: This section specifies that state and county government departments must register and pay fees on behalf of public entities that meet certain criteria, including being funded from the Consolidated Fund and providing public services.
sections.dtia_summary: This section addresses registration obligations for public entities, which may impact DTIA if public entities handle data that requires cross-border transfers.

requirements.text: A state department or county department shall register and pay the fees on behalf of their respective entities
The entities referred to under sub-regulation (1) shall be the public entities at national or county government which operates within a state department or county department
The entities referred to under sub-regulation (1) shall be the public entities at national or county government which is wholly funded from the Consolidated Fund
The entities referred to under sub-regulation (1) shall be the public entities at national or county government which provides a public service
The fees paid by the state department or county department under sub-regulation (1) shall cater for the specified entities registered under the concerned state department or county department

Despite this regulation, a State Corporation or a County Corporation shall be required to register as a data controller or a data processor in respect of their processing activity, in the manner specified under these Regulations

sections.source_quote: A state department or county department shall register and pay the fees on behalf of their respective entities. The entities referred to under sub-regulation (1) shall be the public entities at national or county government which operates within a state department or county department; is wholly funded from the Consolidated Fund; and provides a public service.

sections.heading: Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner must issue a certificate of registration for the duration specified under regulation 9 and enter the applicant's particulars in the register within fourteen days if satisfied with the application's compliance. This applies to data controllers and data processors seeking registration under the Data Protection Regulations.

requirements.text: Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant fulfills the requirements for registration under these Regulations, the Data Commissioner shall, within fourteen days issue the applicant with a certificate of registration for the duration specified under regulation 9
The Data Commissioner shall, within fourteen days enter the particulars of the successful applicant in the register

sections.source_quote: 8. Where the Data Commissioner is satisfied that the applicant fulfills the requirements for registration under these Regulations, the Data Commissioner shall, within fourteen days (a) issue the applicant with a certificate of registration for the duration specified under regulation 9; and (b) enter the particulars of the successful applicant in the register.

sections.heading: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall
sections.summary: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8(a) remains valid for twenty-four months from the date of issuance. This duration applies to data controllers and data processors who have been registered under the Data Protection Regulations.

requirements.text: A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall be valid for a period of twenty-four months from the date of issuance

sections.source_quote: 9. A certificate of registration issued under regulation 8 (a) shall be valid for a period of twenty-four months from the date of issuance.

sections.heading: (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner must notify applicants in writing of refusal within twenty-one days and provide reasons for declining registration applications. Refusal can occur if application particulars are insufficient, privacy safeguards are inadequate, or the entity violates other regulations. Applicants may reapply after complying with refusal requirements.

requirements.text: Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an application for registration, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision notify, in writing, the applicant of the refusal
Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an application for registration, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision provide reasons for such refusal
The Data Commissioner may decline to grant an application for registration, where the particulars provided for inclusion in an entry in the register are insufficient

The Data Commissioner may decline to grant an application for registration, where appropriate safeguards for the protection of the privacy of the data subject have not been provided by the data controller or a data processor
The Data Commissioner may decline to grant an application for registration, where the data controller or data processor is in violation of any provisions of the Act and these Regulations

A data controller or data processor whose application for registration has been declined under these Regulations may make a fresh application upon complying with the requirements specified in the refusal notice

sections.source_quote: 10. (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to approve an application for registration, the Data Commissioner shall within twenty-one days from the date of such decision (a) notify, in writing, the applicant of the refusal; and (b) provide reasons for such refusal.

sections.heading: (1) Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data
sections.summary: Registered data controllers or processors must apply for renewal of registration after their certificate expires. Renewal applications require Form PR 2 and a renewal fee. The Data Commissioner may require verification if the renewal pertains to distinct purposes or data categories.

requirements.text: Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data controller or data processor shall apply for a renewal of registration as a data controller or data processor, after the expiry of the certificate of registration
An application for renewal of a certificate of registration shall be made in Form PR 2 set out in the First Schedule
An application for renewal of a certificate of registration shall be accompanied by the appropriate renewal fee specified in the Second Schedule
The Data Commissioner shall, upon receipt of the application, and where satisfied that the applicant complies with the requirements for registration, renew the certificate of registration

Despite sub-regulation (2), where renewal is for a distinct purpose or categories of data other than that for which the data controller or data processor had been registered for, the Data Commissioner shall undertake a verification process in the manner provided under regulation 7

sections.source_quote: 11. (1) Pursuant to section 20 of the Act, a registered data controller or data processor shall apply for a renewal of registration as a data controller or data processor, after the expiry of the certificate of registration.

sections.heading: (1) Where the Data Commissioner declines to renew an
sections.summary: This section specifies that businesses processing genetic data must register with the Data Commissioner under the Data Protection Regulations, establishing a regulatory threshold for genetic data processing activities.
sections.dtia_summary: This section identifies genetic data as a category requiring specific registration, which is relevant for DTIA as it affects how genetic data is handled across borders and with third parties.

requirements.text: Businesses that process genetic data must register with the Data Commissioner

sections.heading: (1) For purposes of this regulation
sections.summary: Entities with annual turnover below five million shillings and fewer than ten employees are exempt from mandatory registration. However, this exemption does not apply to entities processing personal data for purposes specified in the Third Schedule, and they must still comply with Part IV and Part VI of the Act.

requirements.text: A data controller or data processor is exempt from mandatory registration under these Regulations where the data controller or data processor has an annual turnover of below five million shillings or annual revenue of below five million shillings
A data controller or data processor is exempt from mandatory registration under these Regulations where the data controller or data processor has less than ten employees

Despite the provisions of sub-regulation (2), the data controller and data processor exempt under sub-regulation (2) shall be required to comply with the provisions of the Part IV and Part VI of the Act

The exemption provided under sub-regulation (1) shall not apply to a data controller or data processor whose annual turnover is below five million shillings and processes personal data for the purposes specified under the Third Schedule

sections.source_quote: 13. (2) A data controller or data processor is exempt from mandatory registration under these Regulations where the data controller or data processor (a) has an annual turnover of below five million shillings or annual revenue of below five million shillings; and (b) has less than ten employees.

sections.heading: (1) Subject to section 21 of the Act, the Data Commissioner
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner must maintain an up-to-date register containing registered data controllers and processors, their categories of personal data processed, business addresses, data protection officers (where applicable), and other relevant particulars. This register is updated within 14 days of any changes to registration details and published on the official website every 30 days.
sections.dtia_summary: This section establishes a mandatory registration system that requires data controllers and processors to disclose their data processing activities to the Data Commissioner, creating a foundational record for assessing data flows and ensuring transparency in data handling practices.

requirements.text: The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date register which shall contain the names and particulars of registered data controllers and data processors
The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date register which shall contain categories of personal data being processed by the data controllers and data processors
The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date register which shall contain the address of the principal places of business of the data controllers and data processors
The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date register which shall contain where applicable, details of data protection officers
The Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date register which shall contain any other relevant particular
The Office shall, once every thirty days, publish on the official website a list of registered data controllers or data processors

sections.source_quote: 14. (1) Subject to section 21 of the Act, the Data Commissioner shall keep and maintain an up to date register which shall contain (a) the names and particulars of registered data controllers and data processors; (b) categories of personal data being processed by the data controllers and data processors; (c) the address of the principal places of business of the data controllers and data processors; (d) where applicable, details of data protection officers; and (e) any other relevant particular.

sections.heading: (1) Subject to section 19(2) of the Act, a data controller or
sections.summary: Data controllers and processors must notify the Data Commissioner in writing within 14 days of any changes to their registration particulars. The Data Commissioner may request additional documents or proof before updating the register, and failure to comply constitutes an offence punishable under section 73 of the Act.
sections.dtia_summary: This section mandates timely updates to the registration register, ensuring the Data Commissioner maintains accurate records of data processing activities, which is critical for DTIA compliance as it requires ongoing verification of data flows.

requirements.text: A data controller or data processor shall, within fourteen days of the occurrence of any changes in the particulars of a data controller or a data processor, notify the Data Commissioner in writing
The Data Commissioner shall, on receiving the notification make the necessary changes on the register, where necessary
The Data Commissioner may prior to making any change on the register, request for any necessary documents or proof thereof
A data controller or data processor who contravenes this regulation commits an offence and shall, on conviction, be liable to the penalty specified under section 73 of the Act

sections.source_quote: 15. (1) Subject to section 19(2) of the Act, a data controller or data processor shall, within fourteen days of the occurrence of any changes in the particulars of a data controller or a data processor, notify the Data Commissioner in writing.

sections.heading: (1) Subject to section 22 of the Act, the Data Commissioner
sections.summary: The Data Commissioner may cancel or vary a certificate of registration if a data controller or processor provides false information, willfully or negligently fails to comply with the Act and Regulations, or applies for cancellation/variation. The process must follow the Fair Administrative Actions Act, 2015.
sections.dtia_summary: This section provides the mechanism for revoking registration when data processing activities are non-compliant, which is essential for DTIA to ensure that only legitimate data flows are maintained.

requirements.text: The Data Commissioner may cancel a certificate of registration or vary the conditions for registration, where (a) the data controller or data processor applies for cancellation or variation

The Data Commissioner may cancel a certificate of registration or vary the conditions for registration, where (b) the Data Commissioner establishes that the data controller or data processor provided false or misleading information in relation to any registration particulars

The Data Commissioner may cancel a certificate of registration or vary the conditions for registration, where (c) the data controller or data processor willfully or negligently, fails to comply with provisions of the Act and any Regulations made thereunder
The Data Commissioner shall, before cancelling or varying the conditions of registration, be guided by the provisions of the Fair Administrative Actions Act, 2015

sections.source_quote: 16. (1) Subject to section 22 of the Act, the Data Commissioner may cancel a certificate of registration or vary the conditions for registration, where (a) the data controller or data processor applies for cancellation or variation; (b) the Data Commissioner establishes that the data controller or data processor provided false or misleading information in relation to any registration particulars; or (c) the data controller or data processor willfully or negligently, fails to comply with provisions of the Act and any Regulations made thereunder.

sections.heading: A data controller or a data processor who
sections.summary: Data controllers and processors who fail to register, provide false information, or fail to renew their certificate of registration after expiry commit an offence under section 73 of the Act and face penalties for non-compliance.
sections.dtia_summary: This section defines specific offences related to registration compliance, which are critical for DTIA as they establish the consequences of non-compliance with registration requirements for data processing activities.

requirements.text: A data controller or a data processor who processes personal data without registering in accordance with these Regulations commits an offence
A data controller or a data processor who provides false or misleading information for the purpose of registration commits an offence
A data controller or a data processor who fails to renew a certificate of registration and continues to process personal data after the expiry of the certificate commits an offence
A data controller or a data processor who commits an offence shall, upon conviction, be liable to penalty specified under section 73 of the Act

sections.source_quote: 18. A data controller or a data processor who (a) processes personal data without registering in accordance with these Regulations; (b) provides false or misleading information for the purpose of registration; or (c) fails to renew a certificate of registration and continues to process personal data after the expiry of the certificate, commits an offence and shall, upon conviction, be liable to penalty specified under section 73 of the Act.

sections.heading: Businesses that process genetic data.
sections.summary: This section specifies that businesses processing genetic data must register with the Data Commissioner under the Data Protection Regulations, establishing a regulatory threshold for genetic data processing activities.
sections.dtia_summary: This section identifies genetic data as a category requiring specific registration, which is relevant for DTIA as it affects how genetic data is handled across borders and with third parties.

requirements.text: Businesses that process genetic data must register with the Data Commissioner
